spec.user_story: As a player, I want to analyze different move variations to improve my understanding of the game.

steps.then: The player can see different move sequences on the board.

steps.when: The player selects a move in the variation tree.
steps.then: The board updates to show the selected variation.

states.name: default
states.description: The game is in normal play mode without any variations displayed.

states.name: analysis
states.description: The game is in analysis mode with the variation tree visible.

edge_cases: Selecting a variation with a cycle.
Analyzing a move with no subsequent variations.

inferred_api.hint: Real-time updates for move selections and analysis.

plan: Implement the UI component for the variation tree.
Integrate the variation tree with the board update logic.

acceptance: Verify the variation tree displays correctly in analysis mode.
Ensure selecting a variation updates the board state.

e2e.name: Variation Selection
steps: Start a game and enter analysis mode.
Select a move in the variation tree.
Verify the board updates to the correct state.

unit_or_integration: Test board updates on variation selection.

reasons: Feature is visible and functional in screenshots.
Commonly used in Go game analysis.
open_questions: How does the feature handle very large trees?
Are there performance impacts with real-time updates?
